name: Clobber
author: Albert, Grossman, Nowakowski & Wolfe (2001) / AGP port
description: Clobber (Albert, Grossman, Nowakowski & Wolfe, 2001) — a combinatorial game. The board starts COMPLETELY FILLED in a checkerboard of the two players' stones. On a turn you pick one of your stones and move it onto an orthogonally adjacent OPPONENT stone, clobbering (removing) it. There are no other moves and no draws. Normal play: the player who cannot move loses (last to move wins). Every move removes exactly one stone, so play always terminates.
